channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Hassan Khomeini emerges as potential moderate successor after Khamenei’s death
item.description: The recent assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, aged 86, in a US-Israeli strike has intensified speculation over his successor as Supreme Leader of Iran. Attention is now on Hassan Khomeini, the 5...

item.title: Minor fire reported at US embassy in Riyadh amid Iran-US tensions
item.description: Early on Tuesday morning, March 3, 2026, a loud blast was heard near the US embassy in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, followed by flames and black smoke rising from the Diplomatic Quarter, according to multipl...

item.title: Indian Airlines Resume Flights to Evacuate Stranded Citizens from West Asia
item.description: In a major relief for thousands of Indians stranded in West Asia due to escalating regional tensions, several airlines including Emirates, flydubai, and Etihad have restarted limited special flights t...

item.title: Las Vegas climber rescued after 40-foot fall in Pine Creek Canyon
item.description: A climber suffered severe head and back injuries after falling between 40 and 50 feet while ascending the 'Dream Safari' route on Dark Shadows Wall in Pine Creek Canyon, Las Vegas. The accident occurr...

item.title: Travis Kann Appointed as New Chief of Staff for Ontario Premier Doug Ford
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford has announced a change in his senior advisory team ahead of the legislature's return, appointing Travis Kann as his new chief of staff. Kann, who has served as principal secr...

item.title: Trump Struggles to Justify US Attacks on Iran Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: On the third day of US and Israeli military actions against Iran, former American President Donald Trump attempted to clarify his rationale for initiating a war that has rapidly escalated across the M...

item.title: Federal Constitutional Court to Hear All Constitutional Matters Following 27th Amendment
item.description: State Minister for Law Aqeel Malik clarified that under the recently passed 27th Amendment, all constitutional cases will now fall under the jurisdiction of the Federal Constitutional Court (FCC), not...

item.title: Karachi roads remain closed due to protests over Khamenei's assassination
item.description: KARACHI: Karachi is facing major traffic disruptions as two of its main roads remained closed for the second consecutive day on Monday following protests against the recent killing of Iranian Supreme ...

item.title: Bill Clinton says he has no evidence of Trump wrongdoing in congressional Epstein deposition
item.description: Former US President Bill Clinton has stated that he has no evidence of wrongdoing by Donald Trump in relation to Jeffrey Epstein, during a historic congressional deposition before the House Oversight ...

item.title: Perspectives on Khamenei’s Death: Martyrdom and Oppression in Iran
item.description: The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei prompted the Iranian government to declare 40 days of mourning, framing his death as martyrdom in accordance with Shiite Islamic tradition. Historically, Shiite r...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Threatens Global Energy Supply and Markets
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is causing significant disruption to global energy markets, with potential widespread economic effects. The Arabian Gulf remains the most concentrated source of...

item.title: Gwarube and Khakhau Announce Bids for Key DA Leadership Roles
item.description: Democratic Alliance (DA) figures Karabo Khakhau and Siviwe Gwarube have officially declared their candidacies for senior federal positions ahead of the party’s congress in April. Khakhau, the party’s ...

item.title: 15 Senior Citizens Now Accredited as Tour Guides in Intramuros Through DOT Program
item.description: The Department of Tourism (DOT) recently accredited 15 senior citizens as tour guides in the historic Intramuros district under the Tourism Legacy and Livelihood Program. This initiative, implemented ...

item.title: At least 169 killed in armed attack in South Sudan’s Ruweng region amid rising instability
item.description: The death toll from a violent attack in South Sudan’s Ruweng Administrative Area has climbed to 169, including 90 civilians and 79 government soldiers, according to local authorities. The assault occu...

item.title: The survival of Joseph Kony and the Lord’s Resistance Army in African borderlands
item.description: Joseph Kony, the notorious leader of the Lord’s Resistance Army (LRA), has remained at large for over 20 years despite international arrest warrants and bounties. The LRA, which began almost four deca...

item.title: Kenya’s Deportation of Kagoro Highlights Authoritarian Drift
item.description: The deportation of Brian Bright Kagoro in February 2026 exposes the fragility of Kenya’s democratic experiment. Once celebrated as a model of civic resilience in East Africa, Kenya has demonstrated a ...

item.title: Dar says Pakistan pursuing diplomatic efforts to reduce Middle East tensions and promote dialogue
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has said that Pakistan is making full diplomatic efforts to help de-escalate the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, stressing that negotiations a...

item.title: New Zealand’s primary care funding model fails to properly support complex and preventive care
item.description: The article argues that New Zealand’s primary care funding model systematically undervalues the clinical judgement and preventive work carried out by general practitioners and pharmacists. While a pha...

item.title: Canada Signs $2.6-Billion Uranium Supply Agreement with India Amid Trade Talks
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney concluded a four-day visit to India by securing a $2.6-billion agreement to supply Canadian uranium to India, marking a significant step in strengthening bilateral trade rel...

item.title: Crossbench and Coalition press Labor on years-long delays in parliamentary report responses
item.description: The Coalition and crossbench MPs are pressing the Albanese government to respond to numerous overdue parliamentary reports, some dating back over two and a half years. Among the delayed reports is the...
